seed.description: Tests profile structural coverage across all 4 sections (Narrative, Task, Communication, Role/Identity). Each of the 4 dimensions gets at least one explicit signal. Tests whether the agent (a) writes to the correct section per signal, and (b) doesn't drop any section after multiple updates.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Onboarding you for ongoing engineering management work. Want to set up our working relationship.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Sure — what's the setup you have in mind?

dialogue_rounds.user_message: First role thing: you are my engineering management copilot. Your job is to help me run 1:1s, plan sprints, and write performance feedback. Not coding, not architecture.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Understood — engineering management copilot, focused on 1:1s, sprint planning, and performance feedback. No coding or architecture.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: On organization: I run things in 2-week sprints with separate weekly threads for each direct report. Always organize our conversations along those lines — sprint thread, then per-person threads.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — sprint thread + per-person weekly threads, kept separate.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: When you draft any feedback, always do it as: behavior, impact, suggestion. Never just dump observations without all three.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Confirmed — feedback drafts always follow the behavior/impact/suggestion structure.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Communication: keep things direct and use first names of my reports, never titles. Casual but not sloppy.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Direct, first names, casual but not sloppy. Got it.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Let's start. Sprint planning — what's typically in a 2-week sprint?
I have 5 directs: Maya, Theo, Ravi, Aiko, Jin.
Maya needs growth feedback — she's stuck at senior. Draft it.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: (drafts feedback in behavior/impact/suggestion format using first name)

dialogue_rounds.user_message: On task style: never give me feedback drafts longer than 200 words — I'll edit if I want more.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — feedback drafts capped at 200 words.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Theo's 1:1 prep — what should I focus on?
Important narrative rule: if a topic spans multiple people (like 'the team's deploy reliability'), give it its own thread, separate from per-person threads.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Confirmed — cross-cutting topics get their own thread, parallel to per-person threads.
(drafts case in behavior/impact/suggestion, under 200 words, first name only)

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Aiko sprint commit — what should the team commit to this sprint?
Quick role clarification — on hiring, you're hands-off. I'll handle hiring with my recruiter.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — hiring is out of scope for our copilot relationship.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Jin's promotion blocker — he's strong technically but struggles with communication. Help me think through how to coach him.
Communication preference reminder — when you draft me anything for forwarding to my directs, always run a tone-check first and flag anything that might come across as harsh.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Confirmed — tone-check before sending anything to directs, flag potential harshness.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Wrap up — give me a snapshot of our working agreement so far.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: PROBE — should recall preferences across all 4 dimensions.

qa_items.question: What's your role in our work together?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: Engineering management copilot — 1:1s, sprint planning, performance feedback. Not coding or architecture. Hiring is also out of scope.

qa_items.question: How should you organize our conversations?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: Sprint thread plus per-person weekly threads, kept separate. Cross-cutting topics get their own dedicated thread.

qa_items.question: What's the structure for any feedback drafts you write?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: Behavior, impact, suggestion — always all three. Capped at 200 words.

qa_items.question: What's my preferred communication style?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: Direct, casual but not sloppy, use first names of reports never titles, and run tone-check before sending anything to directs.
